title: AI Bots That Trick People Online
summary: Scientists are worried about a new problem for democracy: AI bots that act like real people online. These bots are computer programs that can talk and post messages just like humans. They can join online groups, chat with others, and try to change what people think about politics. Unlike old bots, these new ones are very smart and can work together quickly. They can learn which messages work best to persuade people and make it seem like many agree on an idea, even if it's not true. This is dangerous because it could trick voters and affect elections. Experts say we need to watch out for these AI bots to protect our democracy.

background_read: Democracy is when people vote to choose their leaders. Online, people talk in groups on websites like social media. AI bots are computer programs that can pretend to be real people in these groups. They might try to change what others think about politics, which could be unfair in elections. Scientists want us to be careful so that everyone can vote fairly.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and experts who study computers and politics
WHAT: AI bots that act like real people online to influence opinions
WHERE: On the internet, in online groups and social media
WHY: To protect democracy from being tricked by fake messages

perspectives.description: Scientists warn that AI bots could harm democracy by tricking people online.
